channel.title: Home on Hanlin Ma
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Hanlin Ma

item.title: What Makes Shutdown Possible? The Significance for AGI of a Shutdown Mechanism with the Data "Market" as an External Constraint
item.description: This page records an association seminar talk by Hanlin Ma. The linked Bilibili page hosts the original video recording.

item.title: A Specialized NARS-P2P Consensus Network Model: An "Anti-Jitter" AGI Community
item.description: This page records Hanlin Ma’s presentation at the Sixth China Annual Conference on Artificial General Intelligence. The linked Bilibili video preserves the original conference recording.

item.title: The Significance of the Second-Person Perspective for Humanoid AI: Emulation, Simulation, and Imitation
item.description: This page records Hanlin Ma’s presentation at the Fifth China Annual Conference on Artificial General Intelligence. The linked Bilibili page preserves the original conference video.

item.title: Limits of AI Moral Enhancement
item.description: This page records the conference presentation listed in the current CV. The present entry uses a year-level placeholder date and can be revised when a more precise event date is available.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hanlin Ma is Associate Professor in the Department of Politics, School of Marxism, Suzhou University of Science and Technology, China.

His research interests include philosophy of science and technology, moral philosophy and ethics, philosophy of cognitive science, philosophy of technology, and the political philosophy of One Health. His recent work also addresses artificial intelligence, moral enhancement, free will, democratic education, and interdisciplinary teaching.

item.title: AI
item.description: Pages tagged with AI.

item.title: An Ethical Analysis of Moral Enhancement: A Free-Will Perspective
item.description: This article develops a free-will-centered analysis of moral enhancement and considers the ethical implications of interventionist approaches to moral agency.

item.title: Bringing Web 3.0 and DAO into Democratic Class: A Study of Pedagogy in Higher Education
item.description: This paper reflects an interest in technology, governance, and higher-education pedagogy.

item.title: Building an Interdisciplinary Knowledge Cultivation System: The Case of PPE Education
item.description: This article reflects on PPE education as a model for interdisciplinary knowledge formation and curriculum design in higher education.

item.title: Cognitive Science
item.description: Pages tagged with cognitive science.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Email is the preferred means of contact:

  • 2439@usts.edu.cn (institutional)
  • fanfan2011cn2000@aliyun.con (common email; for communication in Chinese)
  • fanfan2011cn2000@gmail.com (common email; for communication in English)

Institutional affiliation:

item.title: Conversations on Technology, Ethics, and Society
item.description: A placeholder series for interviews, public conversations, and recorded discussions related to technology, ethics, and society.

item.title: Cyberpunk and Cypherpunk: A Philosophical Analysis Comparing Two Views of the Metaverse
item.description: This conference paper compares cyberpunk and cypherpunk perspectives as rival philosophical frameworks for interpreting the metaverse.

item.title: Governance

item.title: Life-Care Education Based on Improved Mental Models
item.description: Edited volume on life-care education and improved mental models.

item.title: Limits of AI Moral Enhancement
item.description: This article addresses the conceptual and ethical limits of AI-driven moral enhancement and situates the debate within broader concerns about autonomy and technological intervention.

item.title: Meaning of Life and One Health
item.description: Edited volume on life, meaning, and the One Health framework.
